sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 18.97 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.18)

statement.latex: Let$G$be a periodic Zassenhaus group, that is, a two-transitive permutation group with trivial stabilizer of every three points. Suppose that the stabilizer of a point is a Frobenius group with locally finite kernel$U$containing an involution. Is it true that$U$is a$2$-group? This was proved for finite groups by Feit. %�.�. ������}
